_comment: Per-infra-check invocation for the scheduled runner. Copy to infra.config.json on the scheduled host and fill the real endpoints. $ENV tokens are expanded by run.mjs from the host environment so NO secret sits in this file. Real endpoints/domains below are the ones probed from the live studio stack.

ssrf._note: The scrape path is Firecrawl reached via n8n. Explicit SSRF/egress config was not found in the repo during probing — a live run may return unknown until a probe-able scrape endpoint is wired. The deterministic proof is the bundled mock fixtures.

webhook-auth._note: n8n inbound webhook; HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body in header x-n8n-signature. TE-16: the verdict and email-report routes were unsigned and need HMAC + a replay window.

dns-auth._note: Resend sending domain (TE-15 verified DKIM/SPF/MX live). NOTE the container-vs-public DNS trap: a live run may return unknown in-container even when records exist publicly. Run this from a host whose resolver sees public DNS.

supabase-logging._note: Logging/detection config check. Supply a state document — { auth_logs_enabled, api_logs_enabled, log_drain_configured } — fetched from the Supabase management API into a file the host points $SUPABASE_LOGGING_STATE_JSON at. No state => unknown, never a silent pass. (For deterministic offline proof: fixtures/supabase-logging/good/state.json.)

gh-secret-scanning._note: State document { secret_scanning_enabled, push_protection_enabled } from the GitHub repo security-settings API. Detection-config complement of the repo-surface secret-leak check. No state => unknown.

device-signin-alerts._note: State document { google_new_device_alerts_enabled, github_new_device_alerts_enabled } from the apex Google + GitHub account-security settings. No state => unknown.

vercel-observability._note: State document { observability_enabled, firewall_enabled } from the Vercel project settings API. No state => unknown.

alert-route._note: STUBBED — the notify/ alert channel is not wired (n8n workflow pending). With no --notifier it runs against the real notify/ stub and reports `unknown` BY CONSTRUCTION (never a pass) until a test event is watched to arrive. Wire n8n behind notify/notify.mjs, then point a live --notifier at the real route. See notify/README.md.

matrix-freshness._note: No args: fetches the pinned source in mapping/ATTACK_VERSION and compares. Stale is a maintenance finding, never a security alarm or a deploy block.
